skill.name: telegram-mini-app
skill.description: Guides development of Telegram Mini Apps, including Web App API setup, React integration, TON Connect, payments, referrals, and mobile-first UX patterns.
skill.summary: Build Telegram Mini Apps with Web App API, TON Connect, payments, and Telegram-native UX guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby-style backticks and weak cryptography are false positives caused by Markdown fences and ordinary prose. The skill is not malicious, but it gives security-sensitive Telegram, payment, and TON examples, including client-side use of initDataUnsafe without a complete validation pattern.

medium_findings.title: Client-Side Telegram initData Is Shown Without Validation
medium_findings.description: The examples read tg.initDataUnsafe.user and expose user data before showing a server-side validation flow. The file later warns that not validating initData is high severity, so copied examples could encourage trusting spoofable client data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill directly demonstrates initDataUnsafe access and separately identifies missing initData validation as high severity. This is a documentation safety issue, not confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Payment and TON Transaction Examples Lack Validation Guardrails
medium_findings.description: The skill includes Telegram Stars and TON transaction examples but does not pair them with server-side checks for payment payloads, recipient addresses, or amounts. This can lead implementers to copy payment flows without adequate validation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples are legitimate for the skill domain, but the surrounding guidance omits important payment validation steps. The risk depends on how users adapt the examples.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Findings Are Markdown Code Fences
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick locations are Markdown code fences and formatting markers in an instructional document. No evidence found of executable Ruby backticks or hidden command execution in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited patterns are visible Markdown fences or inline formatting, not executable source code. The only command-like example is a normal npm install instruction.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Expected Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The network findings are the official Telegram Web App script, placeholder application URLs, and Telegram share links. No evidence found of credential exfiltration, tracking endpoints, or unauthorized network calls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs match the Telegram Mini App domain context or use clear placeholders. They do not send secrets or point to suspicious third-party collection services.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high static findings for weak cryptography occur in ordinary prose or headings, not in cryptographic API usage. No evidence found of DES, MD5, SHA-1, or custom cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic implementation or weak algorithm reference at the cited lines. The scanner appears to have matched substrings inside non-security text.

dangerous_patterns.title: Use of initDataUnsafe in Authentication Context
dangerous_patterns.description: The examples access Telegram initDataUnsafe user fields on the client. This pattern should only be used for display after signed initData is validated by a trusted backend.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code examples clearly access initDataUnsafe. The security impact is well understood for Telegram Mini Apps when this data is trusted without validation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Client-Side TON Transaction Construction
dangerous_patterns.description: The TON example constructs a transaction using component inputs for amount and recipient. Production apps should validate transaction intent and destination before prompting wallet approval.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is normal for TON Connect examples but can be unsafe if copied without validation. The file does not show malicious destination substitution.

content.value_statement: Telegram Mini Apps require platform-specific UX, authentication, and payment patterns. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design practical Mini App flows with Telegram Web App API and TON guidance.
actual_capabilities: Shows basic Telegram Web App API setup for HTML and React apps.
Explains how bots can launch Mini Apps through inline keyboard web_app buttons.
Provides TON Connect setup patterns for wallet connection and transaction prompts.
Outlines Telegram Stars invoice usage for premium access flows.
Identifies Mini App UX anti-patterns for mobile layout, themes, and loading states.
Suggests retention and referral mechanics for Telegram-native growth.
limitations: Does not provide a complete backend implementation for validating Telegram initData.
Payment examples are illustrative and need production validation and fraud controls.
TON examples assume familiarity with wallet manifests and blockchain transaction review.
Uses placeholder URLs that developers must replace with their own verified domains.

use_cases.title: Plan a Telegram Mini App MVP
use_cases.description: Define the first app structure, launch flow, and Telegram-specific screens before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Add TON Wallet Features
use_cases.description: Design wallet connection, manifest setup, and transaction prompts for a Web3 Mini App.

use_cases.title: Improve Mini App Retention
use_cases.description: Review mobile UX, loading states, referral flows, and Telegram-native interaction patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Mini App Outline
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning a new Mini App
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a Telegram Mini App for [idea]. Include the launch flow, main screens, Web App API needs, and mobile UX risks.

prompt_templates.title: Design the Bot Launch Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Connecting a bot to a web app
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Telegram bot flow that opens my Mini App at [domain]. Include menu entry points, inline buttons, and user onboarding steps.

prompt_templates.title: Review Payments and Wallets
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Telegram Mini App monetization plan. Compare Telegram Stars, in-app purchases, and TON Connect for [product type].

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Production Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Telegram Mini App architecture for production readiness: [architecture]. Focus on initData validation, payments, TON transactions, loading states, and mobile UX.

output_examples.input: Plan a Mini App for a Telegram fitness challenge.
output_examples.output: A compact product plan with bot entry points, onboarding screens, daily check-in flow, leaderboard mechanics, and Telegram theme requirements.

output_examples.input: Add TON Connect to a premium content Mini App.
output_examples.output: A wallet integration checklist covering manifest hosting, connect button placement, transaction confirmation, error states, and validation responsibilities.

output_examples.input: Review my Mini App for Telegram UX issues.
output_examples.output: A prioritized review of mobile layout, theme matching, loading feedback, navigation, touch targets, and retention mechanics.

best_practices: Validate Telegram initData on a trusted backend before granting access or linking accounts.
Use Telegram theme parameters and mobile-first layouts from the first screen.
Add explicit payment, wallet, and transaction review steps before production launch.
anti_patterns: Trusting initDataUnsafe as proof of identity without server-side validation.
Copying placeholder payment or wallet examples directly into production.
Designing a desktop-first web app that ignores Telegram viewport and theme behavior.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps design and implement Telegram Mini Apps with Web App API, bot launch flows, TON Connect, payments, and mobile UX patterns.

faq.question: Can it create a complete production backend?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples, but developers must implement backend validation, storage, monitoring, and deployment.

faq.question: Does it support TON blockchain features?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes TON Connect guidance for wallet connection, manifests, and transaction prompts.

faq.question: Are the payment examples production ready?
faq.answer: No. They are starting points and require validation, fraud controls, logging, and platform compliance review.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: What is the main security caution?
faq.answer: Do not trust Telegram initDataUnsafe for authentication until signed initData is validated by a trusted backend.
